case_title: ESR1 INVESTMENTS LLC, et al VS. JOSEPH TIERNEY et al

docket_entries.description: 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ESR1 INVESTMENTS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY SST INVESTMENTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY AS TO DEFENDANT TIERNEY, JOSEPH DOES 1 TO 100
REPLY TO OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS FILED BY DEFENDANT TIERNEY, JOSEPH
POS OF REPLY TO OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS AND SUPPLEMENTAL DEC FILED BY DEFENDANT TIERNEY, JOSEPH

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JOSEPH TIERNEY'S (AMENDED) MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AND STAYING JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. THE CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION CLAUSE IN THE AGREEMENT TO PURCHASE AND SELL PROPERTY FOR A 50% INTEREST IN THE RIGHT, TITLE AND INTEREST IN PROPERTY DEFINED AS 1250 MISSOURI STREET, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CCP 1298(C). THE AGREEMENT IS EXPRESSLY ONE FOR THE CONVEYANCE OF REAL PROPERTY, AND ACCORDINGLY 1298 APPLIES. DEFENDANT DOES NOT CONTEST THAT THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT HERE DOES NOT COMPLY WITH 1298. JUDGE KARNOW DISCLOSES THAT IN EXCESS OF EIGHT YEARS AGO ON APPROXIMATELY TWO OCCASIONS HE WAS RETAINED AND HIS LAW FIRM WAS PAID BY ATTORNEY WILLIAM FARRER, AND PERHAPS BY MR FARRER'S OPPOSING COUNSEL, TO MEDIATE CASES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-25-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

(Amended) Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Contractural Arbitration And Staying Judicial Proceedings And Pos
Off Calendar Jul-25-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ross McLauran Madden, Esq. of the Law Offices of Ross Madden, Tel. 415.567.1212, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOSEPH TIERNEY's (Amended) MOTION For Order Compelling Contractual Arbitration And Staying Judicial Proceedings is Denied. The contractual arbitration clause in the Agreement to Purchase and Sell Property for a 50% interest in the right, title and interest in property defined as 1250 Missouri Street, San Francisco, California does not comply with CCP 1298(c). The Agreement is expressly one for the conveyance of real property, and accordingly 1298 applies. Defendant does not contest that the arbitration agreement here does not comply with 1298. Judge Karnow discloses that in excess of eight years ago on approximately two occasions he was retained and his law firm was paid by Attorney William Farrer, and perhaps by Mr Farrer's opposing counsel, to mediate cases. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
